notes:
A big pass on the git Changes panel, plus a third Pull Requests tab and a resizable sidebar.

Changes panel, rebuilt Zed-style: each file now leads with a colored status letter (A new, M modified, D deleted, R renamed) instead of a chevron and text badge, the row dividers are gone for a cleaner column, and each file shows a clear + N  − N ledger. Split-diff filler lines are readable again, the active row lost its orange tint, and the panel toggle wears a proper git-branch icon.

Pull Requests: a new Following tab sits next to Mine and For my review; cards now hug their content (no more empty gap at the bottom) and a long title clamps to two lines instead of stretching the card.

Jira: hand an issue straight to the pane's agent to start planning it.

Also: the left sidebar is resizable and remembers its width, ⌘R opens and closes the right panel in one press, and Google sign-in works in the in-app browser.
